title: WhatsApp Business API tokens should not be disclosed
description: WhatsApp Business API tokens enable programmatic access to send and receive messages through the WhatsApp Business Platform. These tokens allow sending messages to customers, managing phone numbers, accessing message templates, and configuring webhooks. Exposure enables unauthorized messaging, brand impersonation, and access to customer conversation data.
recommendation: Remove WhatsApp Business API tokens from source code. Store tokens in environment variables or a secrets manager. Rotate any exposed tokens immediately in the Meta Developer Dashboard.
impact: Unauthorized WhatsApp message sending to customers, brand impersonation, access to customer conversations, and potential spam/phishing campaigns using your verified business number.

checks.message: WhatsApp Business API token detected (EAA prefix — Meta/Facebook token format). WhatsApp Business API tokens grant access to the WhatsApp Business Platform for sending messages, managing phone numbers, and accessing customer conversations. Exposing this token allows attackers to send bulk messages, phishing links, or malware to your customers from your verified business number, damaging customer trust and potentially violating WhatsApp policies. Revoke immediately in the Meta Developer Dashboard.

WhatsApp Business API access token detected in variable assignment. WhatsApp Business tokens enable programmatic messaging to customers and access to the Cloud API or On-Premises API. Unauthorized use can result in mass messaging, customer data access, webhook manipulation, and potential account suspension. Revoke and rotate immediately in the Meta Business Manager.
